vulnerabilities.title: Express-FileUpload Arbitrary File Overwrite
vulnerabilities.description: An arbitrary file write vulnerability in Express-FileUpload v1.3.1 allows attackers to upload multiple files with the same name, causing an overwrite of files in the web application server. This vulnerability is [debated by the package author](https://github.com/richardgirges/express-fileupload/issues/316).

vulnerabilities.title: Prototype Pollution in express-fileupload
vulnerabilities.description: This affects the package express-fileupload before 1.1.8. If the parseNested option is enabled, sending a corrupt HTTP request can lead to denial of service or arbitrary code execution.

vulnerabilities.title: Denial of Service in express-fileupload
vulnerabilities.description:
Versions of `express-fileupload` prior to 1.1.6-alpha.6 are vulnerable to Denial of Service. The package causes server responses to be delayed (up to 30s in internal testing) if the request contains a large `filename` of `.` characters.




## Recommendation

Upgrade to version 1.1.6-alpha.6 or later.
